title: Branding Liability and Governance
summary:
The meeting addressed how to control distribution of branding materials while separating legal liability from an updatable brand rulebook. Participants agreed to divide the current draft into two documents: a signed legal agreement that assigns liability and accountability to agents, and a living branding guideline with a one-page summary that can be updated without re-signature.

Operational rollout and enforcement were discussed in detail, including adding image/video authorization language, embedding the signed legal document into onboarding, using a Loom walkthrough and email trail for distribution, and recording a marketing call to create an audit trail. The group settled on a practical audit cadence—monthly checks for external accounts and ongoing monitoring for proprietary sub-accounts—and a 7–10 day target for approval turnaround. For approvals, Kevin proposed using a custom GPT to perform legal-content reviews and produce consistent, storable compliance reports, while Allie will manually approve non-digital items.

On campaign inclusion and logo use, the team avoided formalizing client additions in the legal doc to limit liability; operations will handle inclusion pragmatically. Social-media rules require logo posts be factual, up-to-date, industry-related, and non-political, with agents responsible for fact-checking and able to consult marketing. Kevin committed to finalize drafts for Allie and legal review, with the group continuing coordination by email.
action_items: Kevin Beeftink will split the current draft into two documents: the branding guidelines and the legal sign-off document.
Kevin Beeftink will implement monthly monitoring/audits for external accounts and set up regular check-ins for proprietary sub-accounts to track compliance.
Allyson Rose Powers will rephrase and present the branding guideline language in a more positive, simplified format for agents.
Matt Ruddick will produce a brief Loom walkthrough and email the signed legal document and summary to the organization to create a distribution record.
Matt Ruddick will have legal reviewers perform a formal legal review of the finalized legal document when the draft is ready.
Kevin Beeftink will create a one-page summary (and potential infographic) that recaps key guidelines for agents after the two documents are finalized.
Allyson Rose Powers will prepare to manually approve non-digital materials and presentations per the agreed scope
Kevin Beeftink will investigate logistics and storage options for the custom GPT compliance workflow and report back
Kevin Beeftink will update the document to set the compliance audit cadence to monthly
Samantha Katchen will configure operational inclusion of the team as a client on campaigns without adding it to the legal rulebook
Mitch will revisit campaign client-inclusion logistics with the team next week to define a practical approach
Kevin Beeftink will split the current document into two parts and produce an initial draft by early next week
Allyson Rose Powers will apply design and formatting to the summary/branding guideline once she receives the final draft content
Matt Ruddick will provide Allie the final draft content needed for formatting (responsibility to supply content)
Kevin Beeftink will perform a final review and finalize a readable draft after the split and AI pass
Matt Ruddick will send the legal document to his lawyer once he receives a near-final version
Kevin Beeftink will send the draft to Allie for design and copy polishing and continue the email chain for follow-up
key_questions: Do you want the legal sign-off to be part of the onboarding process so new hires must sign it?
Should Allyson rephrase the entire document to make it more engaging and easier to read?
What will the legal document contain and whom will it hold accountable?
Which materials will be on Allyson's plate for approval and what is her role?
Can the team specify a turnaround time for approvals and will a GPT be required?
Do we want to be added as a client on every campaign, and should that be in the legal document?
How should political or religious content be handled when an agent uses the KRS logo?
What will Allie’s role be in making documents look presentable versus legal wording?
